The European Union fined Meta 1.2 billion euros for GDPR violations over
cross-border EU-US data transfers. Announced May 22, 2023 by the Irish
Data Protection Commission, it is the largest GDPR penalty ever, exceeding
the 746 million euros previously levied against Amazon. Meta plans to
appeal. The ruling follows a 2020 Court of Justice of the European Union
decision striking down the Privacy Shield framework as inadequate
protection for EU citizens' personal data.
